events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess hazard: PVC flue slope back-pitched (condensing). Heavy industrial, HVAC-R furnace & venting. Work in-progress. Need identify safety hazards, severity, immediate controls. Use references IRC M1801, NFPA 54. Need decision fail because non-compliant/unsafe. Risk high/critical? Back-pitched condensing flue can allow condensate to drain back into furnace/heat exchanger, causing corrosion, flue blockage, CO leakage, combustion issues, potential fire/explosion/carbon monoxide. Severity high maybe critical due to CO and combustion gases. But immediate controls: stop work, shut down furnace, correct slope to drain condensate to termination or condensate drain per manufacturer/code, inspect/replace damaged components, verify vent sizing/materials/clearances, test combustion and CO, obtain inspection. Need fill fields. component_conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete maybe 60? value 0. sound_source irrelevant empty? event_time 0, rate 0, order empty, workable empty. findings list. actions list. rationale. references. Ensure valid JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 60,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "PVC flue is back-pitched, preventing condensate drainage from a condensing appliance vent.",
    "Accumulated condensate can block flue flow, corrode heat exchanger/vent components, and spill combustion gases including CO into the space.",
    "Condition violates required positive slope to drain condensate to an approved point.",
    "Potential for carbon monoxide exposure, combustion failure, and equipment damage."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately shut down and lock out/tag out the furnace.",
    "Stop all related start-up or testing work until the venting is corrected.",
    "Re-pitch the PVC flue so it drains condensate continuously toward the approved termination or condensate drain per manufacturer and code.",
    "Inspect the heat exchanger, vent piping, and fittings for condensate damage or corrosion; replace defective parts.",
    "Verify correct vent material, sizing, support, clearances, and termination before returning to service.",
    "Perform combustion analysis and CO testing after correction; obtain required inspection sign-off."
  ],
  "rationale": "A back-pitched condensing flue traps acidic condensate, which can obstruct venting, corrode components, and cause combustion gases/CO to leak. This is an active safety hazard and a code violation, so the installation fails and must be corrected before the appliance is operated.",
  "references": [
    "IRC M1801 - Chimneys and vents",
    "NFPA 54 - National Fuel Gas Code, venting of gas appliances"
  ]
}
